message:
test(ft-dvgzi): WeztermSubprocessFixture for no-mocks mux integration tests

Builds the foundational fixture for ft-dvgzi (snapshot_e2e migration) and
ft-2funa (watchdog fault injection). Spawns a hermetic wezterm-mux-server
in a TempDir-backed HOME so the global pid lock at
~/.local/share/wezterm/pid (held by the user's interactive wezterm
session) does not block test runs.

New files:
- crates/frankenterm-core/tests/common/wezterm_subprocess.rs (~200 LOC)
  - WeztermSubprocessFixture::spawn() — hermetic HOME + unix domain
    socket inside a TempDir
  - .client() returns a real WeztermClient::with_socket(...) wired
    against the hermetic socket; spawned wezterm CLI subprocesses
    inherit WEZTERM_UNIX_SOCKET (wezterm.rs:2148)
  - .pid() exposes the subprocess id for fault-injection tests in
    ft-2funa
  - Drop reaps the subprocess and removes the TempDir
  - Locates the binary via /usr/bin/which → /opt/homebrew/bin →
    /usr/local/bin
  - should_run() helper gates on FT_REAL_WEZTERM_TESTS=1 so default
    cargo test runs skip cleanly when wezterm-mux-server is absent
- crates/frankenterm-core/tests/wezterm_subprocess_smoke.rs (3 tests)
  - fixture_spawn_returns_socket_path_inside_tempdir
  - fixture_drop_kills_subprocess_and_removes_tempdir (verified via
    `kill -0 <pid>` after Drop)
  - real_wezterm_client_lists_default_pane_via_subprocess — proves
    end-to-end WeztermClient → real mux subprocess → list_panes()
    works; emits structured JSON-line trace per the no-mocks skill

Verified locally:
  FT_REAL_WEZTERM_TESTS=1 \
    scripts/cargo-local.sh test -p frankenterm-core \
    --test wezterm_subprocess_smoke --features asupersync-runtime
  → 19 passed, 0 failed (3 fixture + 16 inherited common-module tests)

Default cargo test (without FT_REAL_WEZTERM_TESTS) takes the skip path
so CI lanes without wezterm-mux-server stay green.

NOT in this commit: the 2058-line snapshot_e2e.rs migration. That work
is structurally non-trivial (MockWezterm and the real mux disagree on
several semantic axes) — surfaced in a follow-up bead comment.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
